CONTENT.Club career: Born in Mankwa, Djeugoué spent his early career with ASPORES and Kadji SA. He then played with Foullah Edifice in Chad, with Douala AC and Coton Sport in Cameroon, and with IR Tanger in Morocco.He was close to signing for Malaysian club Kelantan FA in January 2017, but the deal fell through due to issues with his documentation.After leaving Tanger he played for New Star de Douala, UMS de Loum and Forest Rangers.
CONTENT.International career: Djeugoué made his international debut for Cameroon on 10 August 2013, starting in a 0–1 loss against Gabon.He was selected as part of Cameroon's squad for the 2014 FIFA World Cup, and the 2015 Africa Cup of Nations.

SUMMARY: Cédric Djeugoué (born 28 August 1992) is a Cameroonian international footballer who plays for Forest Rangers as a centre back.
